reasoning: The Texas Senate passed SB 3 in March 2025, which aimed to ban all consumable THC products. However, on April 30, the Texas House State Affairs Committee unanimously overhauled the bill, passing a substitute (CSSB 3) that rejects a total ban and instead seeks to heavily regulate THC products under the Texas Alcoholic Beverage Commission (TABC). The legislative session ends on June 2, 2025, leaving little time for the chambers to reconcile. Given the unanimous resistance to a full ban in the House committee and the strict resolution criteria requiring a 100% full prohibition on the sale of THC (partial bans do not count), it is highly unlikely that a full ban will be enacted into law before the July 1, 2025 deadline.
